'We want to make our voices heard to start a new life': Gaza holds first election in 20 years
subhead: On Saturday, one million Palestinians from the occupied West Bank and 70,000 voters from Deir al-Balah, in the Gaza Strip, were called to the polls to elect their municipal councilors.

title: 40 years after Chernobyl: 'All information related to the accident was classified as top secret'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, historian Galia Ackerman describes what happened before the Chernobyl disaster and the incompetence of the Soviet regime, which deliberately hid the truth about the catastrophe's severity from the public.

title: 'Top Hat' in Paris: A delightful musical with infectious hits
subhead: Inspired by the 1935 film featuring Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers, the show at the Théâtre du Châtelet is directed by Kathleen Marshall, who aims to highlight the African-American roots of jazz and tap.

title: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
subhead: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?

title: How Iranian propaganda lures the West into distraction
subhead: Pro-Iranian accounts are flooding the internet with AI-generated satirical videos that exploit Lego's popularity and Donald Trump's unpopularity, in an attempt to divert Western attention from the suffering of the Iranian people.

title: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
subhead: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.
